Difference between execute(),executeQuerry(),executeUpdate() & executeBatch()

execute()
---
--> it is a general purpose method
--> any type of querry we can execute(SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
--> return type is Boolean
--> SELECT querries are allowed

executeUpdate()
---
--> executeUpdate is use to execute insert, update & delete
--> return type of executrUpdate is int
--> select querry are not allowed


executeQuerry()
---
--> it is used to execute only Select Querry
--

t querry are not allowed


executeQuerry()
---
--> it is used to execute only Select Querry
--> the return type of executeQuerry is result set
--> SELECT querry is allowed


executeBatch()
---
--> it is used to execute multiple DML Querry
--> the return type of executeBatch is int[] array
--> select querry are not allowed


Advantages of jdbc
---
--> jdbc is platform independent , jdbc is a part of java so the code runs on any OS.

--> Database independent :- we can easily s

, jdbc is a part of java so the code runs on any OS.

--> Database independent :- we can easily switch between databases like MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SqL by changing only Driver url not the entire code.

--> Supports multiple sql operations :- jdbc support SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, BATCHEXECUTION , stored procedures and transaction, hence it can perform all the data    base task.

--> Faster in performance, jdbc improve performance by using preparedStaement, batchExecution and co

--> Faster in performance, jdbc improve performance by using preparedStaement, batchExecution and connection pooling.




Servlet
     Servlet is an API or interface which is used to communicate between frontend(Html) to Backend(Java prog, database)
-->> Servlet is an interface or API for which implementation is provided by a java program that runs on a servlet container(Tomcat Server)
     and it is used to create dynamic web pages
-->> Servlet is a part od JEE(java enterprise edition)

and it is used to create dynamic web pages
-->> Servlet is a part od JEE(java enterprise edition) it is responsible for request processing,
     response generation, session management, communication  with database.


Abstract Method that present Inside Servlet Interface
---

interface Servlet {
        void init(ServletConfig);  //-->Use to initialize the servlet/server
        void service(req, resp);   //--> take the request process it and return response
        ServletConfig ge

erit to servlet interface.
                 --> Genericservlet abstract class is protocol independent and we need to override service( )  method.


2)HttpServlet    --> It is a abstract class which inherits to GenericServlet,
                    it is Specially designed to handle Http specific request.
                    it provide http specific request methods like

 			get : fetch data
 			post : sends data or submits data
 			put : for updating the data
 			delete : for deleting t

post : sends data or submits data
 			put : for updating the data
 			delete : for deleting the data

                     --> HttpServlet supports session management.

Q: What is Session Management ?

Difference between Servlet, Generic Servlet, httpServlet

Servlet ; 
it is an interface
it is a packaged in javax.servlet
it is base interface for all types of servlets
it does not support any specific protocol
for all the five abstract method implementation should be provided

t support any specific protocol
for all the five abstract method implementation should be provided i.e init( ), getServletConfig( ), getServletInfo( ), service( ), destroy( )
it is difficult to write code and handle the code
it is rarely implemented directly

	

GenericServlet ; 
it is an abstract class
it is a packaged in javax.servlet
it is protocol independent servlet
it does not support any protocol
default implementation is provided, only service( ) method is abstract in nature

port any protocol
default implementation is provided, only service( ) method is abstract in nature
it is less difficult to write and maintain the code as compare to servlet
it is implemented for non http protocols like Smtp, Ftp etc.


HttpServlet ; 
it is an abstract class
it is a packaged in javax.servlet.http
it is http specific servlet
it supports http protocol i.e GET, POST, PUT, DELETE ETC.
it provides http specific methods i.e doGet( ), doPost( ), doPut( ), doDelete( ) etc..
i

LETE ETC.
it provides http specific methods i.e doGet( ), doPost( ), doPut( ), doDelete( ) etc..
it is very easy to write and maintain the code
it is mostly implemented by all the classes or java web application











sendRedirect()



browser sends a new request to the new URL, URL changes will be seen in the browser

Q. When to use sendRedirected( )
	when we want to go to a website out side of our project.
	you want to redirect after insertion the data.
	you want u

to a website out side of our project.
	you want to redirect after insertion the data.
	you want user to see new URL.


Diffarence between Forward() method and sendRedirected()

forward( )
-----------
forward() method is present in RequestDispatcher interface.
it will happen at server side.
URL does not change in the browser.
faster in execution.
Data is preserved.
Used for internal navigation.
It is more secure.



sendRedirect( )
---------------
sendRedirect() is present i

navigation.
It is more secure.



sendRedirect( )
---------------
sendRedirect() is present in HttpServlet response.
it happens at client side (Browser Side).
URL changes will be seen in the browser.
Slower in execution.
data is lost.
Used for external redirect or avoid form resubmission.
It is less secure.

Title: FloCard Overview
Tags: product, overview, nfc
Type: documentation
----------------------------------------

# FloCard Overview

FloCard is a digital business card platform that allows professionals to share contact information instantly using QR codes and NFC technology.

Key Features:
- Digital profiles
- QR sharing
- Team management
- Analytics dashboard

Title: FloCard Overview
Tags: product, overview, nfc
Type: documentation

Difference between execute(),executeQuerry(),executeUpdate() & executeBatch()

execute()
---
--> it is a general purpose method
--> any type of querry we can execute(SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
--> return type is Boolean
--> SELECT querries are allowed

executeUpdate()
---
--> executeUpdate is use to execute insert, update & delete
--> return type of executrUpdate is int
--> select querry are not allowed


executeQuerry()
---
--> it is used to execute only Select Querry
--

t querry are not allowed


executeQuerry()
---
--> it is used to execute only Select Querry
--> the return type of executeQuerry is result set
--> SELECT querry is allowed


executeBatch()
---
--> it is used to execute multiple DML Querry
--> the return type of executeBatch is int[] array
--> select querry are not allowed


Advantages of jdbc
---
--> jdbc is platform independent , jdbc is a part of java so the code runs on any OS.

--> Database independent :- we can easily s

, jdbc is a part of java so the code runs on any OS.

--> Database independent :- we can easily switch between databases like MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SqL by changing only Driver url not the entire code.

--> Supports multiple sql operations :- jdbc support SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, BATCHEXECUTION , stored procedures and transaction, hence it can perform all the data    base task.

--> Faster in performance, jdbc improve performance by using preparedStaement, batchExecution and co

--> Faster in performance, jdbc improve performance by using preparedStaement, batchExecution and connection pooling.




Servlet
     Servlet is an API or interface which is used to communicate between frontend(Html) to Backend(Java prog, database)
-->> Servlet is an interface or API for which implementation is provided by a java program that runs on a servlet container(Tomcat Server)
     and it is used to create dynamic web pages
-->> Servlet is a part od JEE(java enterprise edition)

and it is used to create dynamic web pages
-->> Servlet is a part od JEE(java enterprise edition) it is responsible for request processing,
     response generation, session management, communication  with database.


Abstract Method that present Inside Servlet Interface
---

interface Servlet {
        void init(ServletConfig);  //-->Use to initialize the servlet/server
        void service(req, resp);   //--> take the request process it and return response
        ServletConfig ge

erit to servlet interface.
                 --> Genericservlet abstract class is protocol independent and we need to override service( )  method.


2)HttpServlet    --> It is a abstract class which inherits to GenericServlet,
                    it is Specially designed to handle Http specific request.
                    it provide http specific request methods like

 			get : fetch data
 			post : sends data or submits data
 			put : for updating the data
 			delete : for deleting t

post : sends data or submits data
 			put : for updating the data
 			delete : for deleting the data

                     --> HttpServlet supports session management.

Q: What is Session Management ?

Difference between Servlet, Generic Servlet, httpServlet

Servlet ; 
it is an interface
it is a packaged in javax.servlet
it is base interface for all types of servlets
it does not support any specific protocol
for all the five abstract method implementation should be provided

t support any specific protocol
for all the five abstract method implementation should be provided i.e init( ), getServletConfig( ), getServletInfo( ), service( ), destroy( )
it is difficult to write code and handle the code
it is rarely implemented directly

	

GenericServlet ; 
it is an abstract class
it is a packaged in javax.servlet
it is protocol independent servlet
it does not support any protocol
default implementation is provided, only service( ) method is abstract in nature

port any protocol
default implementation is provided, only service( ) method is abstract in nature
it is less difficult to write and maintain the code as compare to servlet
it is implemented for non http protocols like Smtp, Ftp etc.


HttpServlet ; 
it is an abstract class
it is a packaged in javax.servlet.http
it is http specific servlet
it supports http protocol i.e GET, POST, PUT, DELETE ETC.
it provides http specific methods i.e doGet( ), doPost( ), doPut( ), doDelete( ) etc..
i

LETE ETC.
it provides http specific methods i.e doGet( ), doPost( ), doPut( ), doDelete( ) etc..
it is very easy to write and maintain the code
it is mostly implemented by all the classes or java web application











sendRedirect()



browser sends a new request to the new URL, URL changes will be seen in the browser

Q. When to use sendRedirected( )
	when we want to go to a website out side of our project.
	you want to redirect after insertion the data.
	you want u

to a website out side of our project.
	you want to redirect after insertion the data.
	you want user to see new URL.


Diffarence between Forward() method and sendRedirected()

forward( )
-----------
forward() method is present in RequestDispatcher interface.
it will happen at server side.
URL does not change in the browser.
faster in execution.
Data is preserved.
Used for internal navigation.
It is more secure.



sendRedirect( )
---------------
sendRedirect() is present i

navigation.
It is more secure.



sendRedirect( )
---------------
sendRedirect() is present in HttpServlet response.
it happens at client side (Browser Side).
URL changes will be seen in the browser.
Slower in execution.
data is lost.
Used for external redirect or avoid form resubmission.
It is less secure.

Exception Handling

Exception: A sudden interruption which stops the flow of execution of the program is known as exception.
           Handling these exception with the help of try and catch is known as exception handling.
	** Exception is an event which stops the execution abnormally and the below line of code will never get executed.

Error : It is also an event which stops the execution of the program but errors can not be handled and exception can be handled.
	Error are of two type

of the program but errors can not be handled and exception can be handled.
	Error are of two type
             ---> Compile time error : Compile time error occurs due to syntax error, Compiler will be aware of compile time error.
             ---> Run-Time Error : Run time error is occurs due to logical problem e.g. Whenever we execute a program without main method we will get an ru                         				   time error known as main method not found error,  



Unchecked Exception :

time error known as main method not found error,  



Unchecked Exception : 
Compiler unknown or unaware exception are unchecked exception 
                         e.g. AerithmaticException
			      ArrayIndexOutOFBoundException
			      StringIndexOutOfBoundException
                              NullPinter exception
                              Class cast Exception

Checked Exception :
 compiler aware or known exception are known as checked exception.

n

Checked Exception :
 compiler aware or known exception are known as checked exception.
                  e.g intruptedException
		      file not foundException
		      ClassNotFoundException

NOTE: All the checked exceptions directly inherit properties from exception class.
      All the unchecked Exception inherits properties from run time exception and runtime exception inherit properties from Exception class.
rties from Exception class.

                  e.g intruptedException
		      file not foundException
		      ClassNotFoundException

NOTE: All the checked exceptions directly inherit properties from exception class.
      All the unchecked Exception inherits properties from run time exception and runtime exception inherit properties from Exception class.
rties from Exception class.

Exception Handling

Exception: A sudden interruption which stops the flow of execution of the program is known as exception.
           Handling these exception with the help of try and catch is known as exception handling.
	** Exception is an event which stops the execution abnormally and the below line of code will never get executed.

Error : It is also an event which stops the execution of the program but errors can not be handled and exception can be handled.
	Error are of two type

of the program but errors can not be handled and exception can be handled.
	Error are of two type
             ---> Compile time error : Compile time error occurs due to syntax error, Compiler will be aware of compile time error.
             ---> Run-Time Error : Run time error is occurs due to logical problem e.g. Whenever we execute a program without main method we will get an ru                         				   time error known as main method not found error,  



Unchecked Exception :

time error known as main method not found error,  



Unchecked Exception : 
Compiler unknown or unaware exception are unchecked exception 
                         e.g. AerithmaticException
			      ArrayIndexOutOFBoundException
			      StringIndexOutOfBoundException
                              NullPinter exception
                              Class cast Exception

 compiler aware or known exception are known as checked exception.
